title: Imperial Knight lightsaber
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Update|[[Legacy (2006) 42|''Legacy'' (2006) 42]], [[Star Wars: Legacy Volume 2]]}} {{Lightsaber |image=[[File:Imperial_Knight_lightsaber.jpg]] |name=Imperial Knight lightsaber |model=[[Lightsaber/Legends|Lightsaber]][[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]] |type=Single-blade |culture=[[Imperial Knight]]s |creator=Constructed by Imperial Knights during their training''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' |created= |destroyed= |discovered= |owners=Every Imperial Knight |hilt model= |hilt shape=Cylindrical, straight |hilt length= |hilt material= |crystal=[[Synthetic lightsaber crystal/Legends|Synthetic lightsaber crystal]] |blade type=[[Bifurcating cyclical-ignition pulse]][[Legacy (2006) 32|''Legacy'' (2006) 32]] |color=[[Color/Legends|White]]-Silver |blade length= |mods=*[[Marasiah Fel]]'s blue-bladed variation[[Legacy (2006) 5|''Legacy'' (2006) 5]] *[[Azlyn Rae]]'s orange-bladed variation[[Legacy (2006) 43|''Legacy'' (2006) 43]] *Ceremonial [[Lightsaber pike/Legends|long-handled]] variation[[Legacy — War 6|''Legacy — War'' 6]] |weight= |protection= |capacity= |range=Personal |design= |markings=Black ridges |precede= |succeed= |purpose=*Protect the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] *Offensive and defensive [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] |affiliation=*[[Fel Empire]][[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]] **Imperial Knights[[Legacy (2013) 1|''Legacy'' (2013) 1]] *[[Empire-in-exile]][[Legacy (2006) 3|''Legacy'' (2006) 3]] **Imperial Knights }} {{Quote|Make sure you can get your saber ''if'' you need it. I don't trust this bunch.|Antares Draco, to Ganner Krieg, while facing down a group of Galactic Alliance bodyguards|[[Legacy (2006) 9|''Legacy'' (2006) 9]]}} The '''Imperial Knight lightsaber''' was a standard [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] model used by the [[Imperial Knight]]s, an order of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-users]] who were loyal to the [[Fel Empire]] and later [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]'s [[Empire-in-exile]]. The uniform design represented both the Imperial Knights' unity and the Empire's sovereignty over each individual Knight. The lightsaber had a [[Color/Legends|gray]], cylindrical [[Lightsaber hilt/Legends|hilt]] that was partially covered by black ridges; save for in customized variations, its angled [[Blade emitter/Legends|emitter]] produced a white blade. Using a [[Synthetic lightsaber crystal/Legends|synthetic lightsaber crystal]], each Imperial Knight constructed his or her own lightsaber during training, following techniques outlined by the former [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] in his [[The Book of Anger|''Book of Anger'']]. Able to function both on land and underwater, the lightsaber was one of several tools the Imperial Knights used to protect their liege, a task that was the core purpose of the order. In [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|combat]] situations, Imperial Knights complemented their offensive lightsaber attacks with defensive [[Cortosis gauntlet/Legends|cortosis gauntlets]], which could disable an enemy's lightsaber blade and allow the Knight to attack. When not using the weapon in combat, an Imperial Knight usually kept the lightsaber clipped to his or her [[Belt/Legends|belt]]. Imperial Knight lightsabers were used by the order as early as [[130 ABY]]. They saw heavy use in [[137 ABY|137]] and [[138 ABY]] during the [[Second Imperial Civil War]], when the Empire-in-exile fought an insurgency against [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]]. Early in 137 ABY, Imperial Knights [[Antares Draco]], [[Ganner Krieg]], and [[Marasiah Fel]] employed their weapons during a [[Skirmish on Vendaxa|battle]] against the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[One Sith]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Vendaxa/Legends|Vendaxa]]. The skirmish saw [[Cade Skywalker]]—a former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who had abandoned his [[Skywalker family/Legends|family's heritage]] to become a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]—pick up Marasiah's lightsaber and attack the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], a move that proved to be his first step in reclaiming his Jedi legacy. Over the next [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Imperial Knights used their lightsabers in battle against Krayt's forces on worlds such as [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]], [[Dac/Legends|Dac]], and [[Had Abbadon]]; Sith [[Death/Legends|fell]] to Imperial blades in the war's final stages on both [[Taivas]] and the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. During the [[Battle of Coruscant (Second Imperial Civil War)|battle]] on Coruscant, Roan Fel attempted to use a [[Omega Red|bioweapon]] to wipe out all life on the planet. Draco was thus prompted to [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|slay]] Fel with his lightsaber, thereby preventing the Emperor from committing an act born of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. ==Description== {{Quote|Jedi?''"
"''Better. We're Imperial Knights.|[[Monia Gahan]], assuming that her lightsaber-wielding saviors are Jedi, is corrected by Sigel Dare|[[Legacy (2006) 22|''Legacy'' (2006) 22]]}} [[File:Nonebetter.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Imperial Knight lightsabers represented the individual Knights as being less than the Empire they served.]] A standard model of [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] known as the [[Imperial Knight]] lightsaber was used by every member of the Imperial Knights, an order of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force users]] who were active during both the [[Sith–Imperial War]] and the [[Second Imperial Civil War]]. Loyal to the [[Fel Empire]] and later the [[Empire-in-exile]], the Imperial Knights were an organization dedicated to the protection of their [[Emperor/Legends|liege]] and the furtherance of their Empire. The common lightsaber design was a symbol both of the order's unity and of their belief that each individual Knight was less than the Empire that they served. The weapon's [[Lightsaber hilt/Legends|hilt]] was [[Color/Legends|gray]] and cylindrical, with black ridges that covered less than half of its curved surface. The [[activation stud]] was placed near the angled [[Blade emitter/Legends|emitter]], which produced a white blade that was able to function both on land and [[Bifurcating cyclical-ignition pulse|under]][[Water/Legends|water]]. Some customized lightsabers produced blades of different colors, such as a blue-bladed weapon carried by [[Marasiah Fel]], an Imperial Knight and the [[Prince/Legends|princess]] of the Empire-in-exile. Imperial Knight [[Azlyn Rae]] once used an orange-bladed variation while [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|sparring]]. During undercover missions, Rae carried a lightsaber of a different make that also produced an orange blade.[[Legacy (2006) 24|''Legacy'' (2006) 24]] An additional variation of the Imperial Knight lightsaber was a [[Lightsaber pike/Legends|long-handled version]]. As tall as a [[Human/Legends|Human]], it had a flat emitter and was ignited during ceremonial occasions. Although the organization used a common model, each Imperial Knight crafted his or her own lightsaber while training in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. The weapons were constructed in a manner similar to that of the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order]]; however, unlike their [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] counterparts, Imperial Knights created [[Synthetic lightsaber crystal/Legends|synthetic lightsaber crystals]] rather than seeking out [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|natural ones]]. Their method of crystal construction was adapted from the writings of [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], a late [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] and [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|ruler]] of the former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Palpatine had detailed the creation of synthetic crystals in ''[[The Book of Anger]]'', a [[Book/Legends|tome]] of [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] lore that nevertheless contained some teachings—such as the crystal construction process—which were free of the dark side's taint. The Imperial Knights' construction process was bereft of ceremony and was afforded little more significance than when a new [[Force power/Legends|Force power]] or fighting technique was learned. Lessons in [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed]] and vehicular combat, as well as in the use of Force powers, required as much of an apprentice's [[Time/Legends|time]] as did [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber training]], and the completed weapon was seen by each Knight as one tool in a larger combat arsenal. Imperial Knights used their lightsabers in the practice of various forms of lightsaber combat, including two that were unique to their order: the aggressive ''[[Praetoria Vonil]]'' and the defensive ''[[Praetoria Ishu]]''. The lightsabers themselves, rather than [[Training lightsaber/Legends|practice weapons]], were utilized during friendly sparring sessions.[[Legacy (2006) 44|''Legacy'' (2006) 44]] In combat situations, Imperial Knights often used their lightsabers to deflect [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] shots fired by opponents until they came close enough to attack.''[[Threats of the Galaxy]]'' They also complemented the use of their lightsaber with defensive [[Cortosis gauntlet/Legends|cortosis gauntlets]]. Worn on their forearms, the [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]]-embedded gauntlets could block the lightsaber strikes of opponents[[Legacy (2006) 46|''Legacy'' (2006) 46]] and disable the blade for several [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]],[[Legacy (2006) 13|''Legacy'' (2006) 13]] in turn allowing the Imperial Knights to use their own lightsabers to counter-attack their momentarily defenseless foe. When not drawn, an Imperial Knights' lightsaber was usually clipped to his or her belt, where it visibly hung atop their [[Imperial Knight armor|crimson armor]].[[Legacy (2006) 35|''Legacy'' (2006) 35]] The combination of white-bladed lightsaber and crimson [[battle armor]] was a prominent trait of the Imperial Knights that visibly distinguished them from their Jedi cousins. By itself, the lightsaber sometimes led spectators to believe that its wielder was a Jedi. ==History== ===Early strikes=== {{Quote|Requiring us to leave our lightsabers on the ship makes guarding the ambassador more difficult.''"
"''Good thing we smuggled them on anyway. Especially knowing our Galactic Alliance friends there have concealed blasters.|Ganner Krieg and Antares Draco, while aboard the Wheel|}} The Imperial Knight lightsaber was in use as early as [[130 ABY]] during the closing stages of the Sith–Imperial War. When the conflict came to an end, Emperor [[Roan Fel]] of the Fel Empire was betrayed by his [[One Sith]] allies. In a ruse, four Imperial Knights used their lightsabers to [[Attack on Emperor Fel|protect]] Fel's [[Roan Fel's double|security double]] when the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Krayt]] arrived on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to usurp the Imperial throne. As Krayt [[Death/Legends|killed]] them with his own blades, the lightsabers of Fel and his loyal Knight [[Antares Draco]] concurrently saw action against [[LV8-series guard droid]]s as the two fugitives escaped through the planet's [[Sublevel 12]].[[Legacy (2006) 8|''Legacy'' (2006) 8]] Displaced from his throne, Fel became the leader of an Empire-in-exile, and his Imperial Knights continued to serve him as he opposed [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]]. At some point prior to [[137 ABY]], Imperial Knight [[Darth Havok|Eshkar Niin]] abandoned the vows he had made to the Emperor and used his lightsaber to [[Rooftop duel|kill]] Fel's [[Marriage/Legends|wife]], [[Elliah Fel|Elliah]], before defecting to Krayt's [[Sith/Legends|Sith]].[[Legacy — War 2|''Legacy — War'' 2]] [[File:ImperialKnightsonVendaxa.jpg|thumb|right|325px|Antares Draco and Ganner Krieg brandish their lightsabers against Sith on Vendaxa.]] The [[Standard year/Legends|year]] 137 ABY saw a heavy amount of conflict between the two Empires in one of the Second Imperial Civil War's most intense periods of fighting,[[Legacy (2006) 6|''Legacy'' (2006) 6]][[Legacy (2006) 31|''Legacy'' (2006) 31]][[Legacy (2006) 36|''Legacy'' (2006) 36]] and lightsaber-equipped Imperial Knights were routinely dispatched on missions throughout the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] by their Emperor.[[Legacy (2006) 23|''Legacy'' (2006) 23]] Early in the year, the Imperial Knight [[Elke Vetter]] employed her lightsaber in a [[Duel on Socorro|duel]] against the Sith [[Hand (One Sith)|Hand]] [[Darth Talon]], but was overpowered and killed by her opponent.[[Legacy (2006) 2|''Legacy'' (2006) 2]] Talon later led a group of Sith to [[Vendaxa/Legends|Vendaxa]] to apprehend Princess Marasiah, who was under the protection of Antares Draco and fellow Imperial Knight [[Ganner Krieg]], as well as [[Wolf Sazen|two]] [[Shado Vao|Jedi]] and the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] crew of the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] [[Mynock (Helot-class)|''Mynock'']]. A number of Sith fell to the three Knights' blades during the subsequent [[Skirmish on Vendaxa|skirmish]].[[Legacy (2006) 7|''Legacy'' (2006) 7]] The [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the ''Mynock'' was [[Cade Skywalker]], a former Jedi and the heir to a [[Skywalker family/Legends|powerful legacy]] who had forsaken his family's Force-using tradition years before and turned to a life of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunting]]. During the battle, his first step toward reclaiming his legacy came when he picked up the lightsaber of a gravely injured Marasiah Fel and used it to attack the Sith. As the war continued to rage, Draco and Krieg were sent by their Emperor to a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] known as [[The Wheel/Legends|the Wheel]] to serve as [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] to Imperial emissary [[Mingo Bovark]] as he negotiated an alliance with [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gar Stazi]]. Although no weapons were allowed on the station, Draco and Krieg smuggled their lightsabers aboard and used them to deflect [[Laser/Legends|laser]] blasts fired by [[Galactic Alliance Intelligence officer|Alliance officers]] when the meeting was [[Skirmish on The Wheel|sabotaged]] by Sith-employed [[Spy/Legends|spies]]. When the two Knights refused to surrender their weapons to Wheel [[Administrator/Legends|Administrator]] [[Pol Temm]], Temm declared the station off-limits to all of Emperor Fel's forces.[[Legacy (2006) 10|''Legacy'' (2006) 10]] The Emperor's own lightsaber later saw action on his Empire's [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]] of [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]] when the Sith [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Darth Kruhl]] [[Duel on Bastion|infiltrated]] the planet and attacked Fel in the [[Pellaeon Gardens]]. Fel used his weapon to deflect the Sith's lightsaber attacks, but when Kruhl's [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] [[Sun djem/Legends|disarmed]] him, the Emperor finished his foe off with a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]]. ===Piercing the Sith=== {{Quote|Is this how you welcome all visitors, Jedi?''"
"Uninvited'' visitors who arrive with ''lightsabers'' in hand, yes!|Ganner Krieg and Shado Vao, while dueling at the Hidden Temple|[[Legacy (2006) 25|''Legacy'' (2006) 25]]}} When Fel sent Imperial Knights [[Treis Sinde]] and [[Sigel Dare]] to the planet [[Dac/Legends|Dac]] to sabotage the construction of an [[Alliance (Imperious-class)|advanced Star Destroyer]] belonging to Krayt's Empire, the two successfully executed their undercover mission in plainclothes but nevertheless carried their lightsabers. Aided by several of the planet's native [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]], Admiral Stazi soon after [[Battle of Mon Calamari (Second Imperial Civil War)|seized]] the [[Destroyer/Legends|destroyer]] in the name of the Galactic Alliance Remnant, and [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Krayt consequently arrived on-world to declare [[Genocide on Dac|genocide]] on the Mon Calamari people. Sinde and Dare, on Dac at the time, used their lightsabers to cut through hordes of Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] on their way to rescue several [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] of the Galactic Alliance Remnant in an effort to warn Stazi of the explosives that they had planted. As Dare and the pilots took a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] into [[Realspace/Legends|space]], Sinde remained on Dac's surface and [[Massacre of New Coral City|dueled]] the Sith Lord [[Darth Azard]], thereby buying his comrades time to escape. After escaping from the Sith Lord, Sinde became trapped on the planet and joined forces with the [[Mon Calamari Rangers]], a resistance cell active against Krayt's Empire. Sinde fought alongside them in [[Skirmish on Dac|several]] [[Battle of Devil's Crevasse|battles]], and often used his lightsaber against Sith Imperial forces while he swam through the planet's [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]].[[Legacy (2006) 33|''Legacy'' (2006) 33]] [[File:Underwaterlightsaber.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Treis Sinde uses his lightsaber underwater while fighting alongside the Mon Calamari Rangers on Dac.]] While Sinde was on Dac, Emperor Fel pursued a formal alliance with the Jedi Order. Marasiah, Draco, and Krieg covertly followed the ''Mynock'' to a [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|Hidden Jedi Temple]] on the planet [[Taivas]], and when the Princess's protectors departed their [[Sigma-class long-range shuttle|ship]] with lightsabers drawn, Skywalker and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] Shado Vao [[Skirmish at the Hidden Jedi Temple|attacked]] them. Tensions died down after a brief lightsaber duel, and Draco and Krieg shortly thereafter joined Vao and the ''Mynock''{{'s}} crew on a [[Mission to Had Abbadon|mission]] to the planet [[Had Abbadon]] to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] Darth Krayt.[[Legacy (2006) 26|''Legacy'' (2006) 26]] Aided by the ancient Jedi Knight [[Celeste Morne]] and her [[Army/Legends|army]] of [[Rakghoul]]s, the group did [[Battle on Had Abbadon|battle]] with Krayt and several of his Sith Lords. A number of the volatile Rakghouls were felled by the Imperial Knights' blades when Morne lost control of them, and Krayt ultimately survived the encounter.[[Legacy (2006) 48|''Legacy'' (2006) 48]] Fel's alliance with the Jedi was only secured when he [[Mission to Agamar|traveled]] to the planet [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]] to conduct peace talks with several Jedi representatives;[[Legacy (2006) 45|''Legacy'' (2006) 45]] more than ten lightsaber-armed Knights accompanied the Emperor to the meeting, and they fought alongside their new Jedi allies when a Sith [[First Sith Imperial Strikeforce|strike force]] [[Operation: Thunderstroke|arrived]]. Many Sith were killed by the blades of the Imperial Knights, who effectively used their lightsabers and cortosis gauntlets to protect their Emperor. Along with the Jedi, they were able to ferry Fel away from the planet. With a three-way alliance secured between the Empire-in-exile, the Jedi, and the Galactic Alliance Remnant, Darth Krayt began a renewed military push against his enemies in [[138 ABY]]. Imperial Knights used their lightsabers against the Sith during [[Battle of Borosk (138 ABY)|battles]] on worlds such as [[Borosk/Legends|Borosk]] and Taivas. Draco, Krieg, Marasiah, and Azlyn Rae stood with the Jedi as they [[Attack on the Hidden Temple|defended]] the Hidden Temple from a Sith onslaught, cutting down several Sith warriors who attempted to breach the temple's gates.[[Legacy — War 3|''Legacy — War'' 3]] Krieg and Rae used their lightsabers to bring down an Imperial [[Walker/Legends|walker]].[[Legacy — War 4|''Legacy — War'' 4]] After Taivas was evacuated, the allies [[Battle of Coruscant (Second Imperial Civil War)|struck]] at Krayt's throneworld of Coruscant, where Rae, Krieg, and Dare joined a [[Cade Skywalker's strike team|strike team]] led by Skywalker that was tasked with the deactivation of the world's [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shields]]. The Imperial Knights used their lightsabers to battle their way through Sith warriors and members of Darth Krayt's elite [[Darth Krayt's Sith trooper|Sith trooper]] army on their way to the planet's central defensive [[Computer/Legends|computers]]. After they arrived, Krieg's lightsaber was one of several which disabled the computers and brought down the planetary shields, which in turn allowed the allied [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] to attack.[[Legacy — War 5|''Legacy — War'' 5]] The Emperor had his own plans to destroy the Sith and, from the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Jagged Fel (starship)|''Jagged Fel'']], prepared to release a [[Omega Red|bioweapon]] onto Coruscant's surface that would destroy all life on the planet. As every Imperial Knight had vowed to kill the Emperor if ever he were to fall to the dark side, Draco engaged Fel in a lightsaber duel. The two Knights locked blades outside an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]], and as Fel prepared to launch the weapon, Draco's blade [[Shiak|pierced]] Fel's chest and killed him. The Sith eventually retreated from Coruscant and, during a [[Funeral of Roan Fel|funeral]] for the Emperor held on-world in the battle's aftermath, Marasiah was declared the new Empress and a member of the [[Galactic Federation Triumvirate]]. Draco and Krieg flanked her, holding ignited long-handled Imperial Knight lightsabers aloft as those in attendance mourned the death of the Emperor. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:IKlightsaberfigure.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Action figure of Antares Draco and lightsaber]] The Imperial Knight lightsaber was first mentioned and pictured in [[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]], a [[June]] [[2006]] preview issue of the upcoming [[Star Wars: Legacy|''Legacy'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series. As recurring characters in ''Legacy'', Imperial Knights went on to frequently wield their lightsabers throughout the series' fifty-issue run, as well as in its follow-up miniseries, [[Star Wars: Legacy — War|''Star Wars: Legacy—War'']]. Further details on the weapon and its method of creation were revealed in [[2009]]'s ''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'', which was also the first source to explicitly name the weapon as the "Imperial Knight lightsaber." In the ''Legacy'' comics, the lightsaber's blade is consistently depicted with a white hue. The ''Legacy Era Campaign Guide'' and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' similarly describe a white blade,{{CSWECite|II|91|Imperial Knight}} but ''[[Threats of the Galaxy]]'' mistakenly identifies the color as silver. The Imperial Knights' lightsaber has been immortalized in plastic on several occasions, a rarity for weapons of the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]]. Miniature figurines of Antares Draco, Ganner Krieg, Sigel Dare, Marasiah Fel, and Roan Fel were released by [[Wizards of the Coast]] in [[2008]] as part of the [[Legacy of the Force (Star Wars Miniatures)|''Legacy of the Force'']] set of [[Star Wars Miniatures]]. All five figures pose with their lightsabers drawn.{{SWMiniCite|set=Legacy of the Force|parent=2}} Additionally, from 2008 to 2009, 3 ¾ inch action figures of Draco, Krieg, and Dare were released by [[Hasbro]]. Draco and Krieg are packaged together with the [[Legacy (2006) 6|sixth issue]] of ''Legacy'', while Dare is packaged with Darth Krayt and comes with [[Legacy (2006) 22|Issue 22]]. Draco and Krieg come with lightsabers with removable blade, while Dare's is fixed.{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Legacy Collection|packtype=''Star Wars'' Comic Pack|pack=#05: Antares Draco & Ganner Krieg (''Legacy'' #6)|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/TLCcomicpacks05.asp}}{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: Legacy Collection|packtype=''Star Wars'' Comic Pack|pack=#08: Darth Krayt & Sigel Dare
